title: The Landscape of Algorithmic Solvability
description: A Venn diagram illustrating the relationship between different classes of computational problems and their solvability by efficient algorithms, highlighting the limits of algorithmic power.
relationships: P is a subset of all problems that are solvable in polynomial time. Intractable problems are outside of P. NP-Complete problems are a class of intractable problems believed to not be in P. Undecidable problems are a separate set entirely, representing problems for which no algorithm can ever exist to solve them.
